g.data-narration: Welcome to Stakeholders in Software Engineering - the key to project success
A stakeholder is anyone who can affect or is affected by project decisions, activities, or outcomes.
g.text: "Any individual, group, or organization that can affect, be affected by, or perceive itself to be
affected by a decision, activity, or outcome of a software project."

g.data-narration: Projects with effective stakeholder engagement are significantly more likely to meet objectives and deliver value.

svg.g:
Why Stakeholder Management Matters
📊 Projects with effective stakeholder engagement = Higher Success
⚠️ Without it: delays, budget overruns, failure
<rect x="530" y="195"220" width="220" height="35" rx="4" fill="#fff"/> 🎯 Ensures project delivers value to all parties

g.data-narration: Stakeholders are classified into Internal (within organization) and External (outside organization) categories.
Internal stakeholders include project sponsor, project manager, development team, upper management, and functional managers.
External stakeholders include customers, users, suppliers, regulators, competitors, and the general public.
Important distinction: Customers BUY the software while Users actually OPERATE it. These are often different people.
The Power/Interest Grid classifies stakeholders based on their influence (power) and level of concern (interest).
g.text: A tool to categorize stakeholders by their Power (influence) and Interest (level of concern)

g.data-narration: Four quadrants: Manage Closely (high power/high interest), Keep Satisfied (high power/low interest), Keep Informed (low power/high interest), Monitor (low power/low interest).
The five-step stakeholder analysis process: Identification, Analysis, Classification, Engagement Planning, and Engagement.
A stakeholder register documents identification info, assessment info, and classification for all stakeholders.
g.text: Document that captures information about all identified stakeholders

g.data-narration: Stakeholder engagement levels: Unaware → Resistant → Neutral → Leading. Goal is to move stakeholders toward active support.
g.text: Measuring current state vs desired state of stakeholder engagement

g.data-narration: Example: Banking software stakeholder analysis includes CEO, IT Director, RBI regulator, customers - each with different classification.
g.text: Internet banking system for a commercial bank

g.data-narration: Healthcare EHR example: Doctors and Administration are high power/high interest; Patients are low power/high interest; Ministry of Health is high power/low interest.
Key exam points: Memorize the Power/Interest Grid quadrants, 5-step analysis process, customer vs user distinction, and stakeholder register contents.
g.text: ✅ Definition: Anyone who can affect or is affected
✅ 5-step Analysis: ID → Analysis → Classify → Plan → Engage

g.data-narration: In summary: Stakeholder management is critical for project success. Identify all stakeholders, classify using Power/Interest Grid, and engage appropriately.
g.text: Stakeholder management is critical for project success. Identify all stakeholders (internal & external),
classify using Power/Interest Grid, and develop appropriate engagement strategies.
